And caught another bug on my last test: current any2 logic assumes that both energies are converted to the same color which is not a valid assumption.  Fix.

## **14 Sept 2022**

I worked on the aforementioned issue a bit yesterday too but had to go before writing anything.  Did a more substantial overhaul of the conversion logic than anticipated, but it’s looking much better.  I am hesitant to say it, but I think the client-side conversion is 100% working.  Haven’t been able to spur any weird edge cases, but I’m sure a deeper dive will uncover some.  But I am at least satisfied for now.

Moving on to server-side.  Design:

1. Submit action passes two things objects:  
   1. Builder.selected\_spheres \- an array of energy IDs that were selected and/or used on converters  
   2. Builder.active\_converters \- the JSON object which describes how each converter was used.  I.e. what color energy and how many were converter to what color energy and how many.  Need to ensure that these are ordered (can easily add a quick order if not)  
2. The PHP then effectively compares the active\_converters and confirms everything that was done is legal and kosher.  For each active\_converter:  
   1. Ensure DB agrees it was built by active player and is a converter  
   2. Ensure the converter can convert the colors and numbers listed  
   3. Ensure an energy of the from color was included  
   4. Ensure that this energy belongs to the active player  
   5. Record the to energy for final summing

This will be fun (no /s I love this shit).  Let’s put it all in a new file.  And divide some of existing game logic into another DB file.

Realizing now that with the advent of “second\_convert” from\_number is always 1\.  I need to remove it or it will now bother me forever.  Was easy.

Have a good start on the conversion validation.  Biggest thing that stands out to me at this point is there’s still nothing stopping someone from selecting all their energy (more than needed) and submitting.  Currently the code would spend it all.  Need to implement some logic that actually recognizes which energy was used.  I think the spend\_power route may need to go for the server-side.  Next time.

## **17 Sept 2022**

New idea for above issue: throw an error if there is extra spend\_power indicating that players cannot overspend.  I like this even more.  It’s a pretty extreme edge case and I don’t want to spend a ton of time/effort handling it.  Works perfectly.

Hit some issues in testing the new PHP code for validating converter logic, but none too hard to fix.  Just missteps around methods being called from a static context with references to class variables.  And then a few more around assigning duplicate orders due to simple logic mistakes.

In all my testing I realized that the game should be ending and it’s not.  So let’s add that.  Oddly enough I already have game progression working and game end is pretty much the same thing \- just check if progression \== 100\.  The other part of it is making that fact obvious to players.  I.e. show a big red message.  I like the one in Splendor so I’m just going to copy paste it.  Works nice.

New bug: the onCardSelect is not being assigned to filed cards.  But why… I think it was because the zone was not being created unless a player actually had any archive (which they never would on initial game creation).  WIll need to retest.

Next issue: the archive/energy/research numbers need to be recalculated regularly for the case where players are playing in one browser session continuously.  Handling this on the onEnteringState for selecting a card/deck with a params function on the state.  This seems to work.

The issue with onCardSelect doing nothing is applying to cards in regular built rows too, but it’s intermittent and a refresh consistently fixes.  Will need to spend more time with that.  Okay I confirmed that one of the issues is with not adding the ‘converter’ class to converters after being built.  This may be isolated from the archive issue as those seem to be working now.  Should also remove the selectable class.  Next time, need some sleep.

## **18 Sept 2022**

Converter selection issue was easy to fix.  Then spent some time fucking around with animations again.  It seems that there is some blocking issue when using animations in tandem with zones.  For next time I try this, the issue I’m seeing is (code is commented out now:

1. Activate a color converter such that it uses an energy from the ring  
2. Animation plays nicely with removing energy from zone and sliding to converter (using onEnd)  
3. Deselect the color converter  
4. Energy can make it back onto the ring, but it is not correctly added to the zone.  I.e. it sits in the upper right corner with the wrong X/Y positioning

May revisit that, but I think not pre-alpha.  Animations are nice, but not *needed*.

Kept running through a game and hit another few state-related bugs (fixed by refreshing).  Some I could not troubleshoot without more logging and are difficult to reproduce.  Summary:

1. In response to selecting a card, getPlayerSpheresOfColor sometimes throws an error when trying to check the class of their energy within sphere\_counts.  I assume this has to do with some operation not correctly spending/removing spheres from the array, but I’m not sure what function that would be.  Need more data  
2. Is that it?

Next I’m adding the logic to calculate value of variable score upgrades at game end.  I really like the way Race for the Galaxy handles this.  For each card that needed to be calculated, generate a notification to all players indicating the score of the card.  Eventually this could get moved to a scoring dialog, but that is likely a lot more effort.

Still working on this \- need to write out the notification logic to tell players how much each scored and why.  Next time if I don’t get back to this tonight.

## **19 Sept 2022**

Finished up the end game notifications and it works great.  Next lets finish up the rest of the level 3 edge cases.  Not too many left and should be much easier than all the converters:

1. Build\_level1\_for0 \- DONE  
   1. New state seems like the best option for this.  It breaks too many usual rules to build it into an existing one.  
2. Research \- Retest \- DONE  
3. Discount\_level2 \- DONE  
4. Discount\_buildfromfile \- DONE  
5. Discount\_buildfromresearch \- DONE  
6. No\_research \- DONE  
7. No\_file \- DONE

Note from the future: I disabled the “random 16 cards” logic on setup for ensuring I can test all these level 3 gizmos.  I have since re enabled it \- was just 2 commented lines around an if block.

## **22 Sept 2022**

Finished up the server-side code for build\_level1\_for0.  Moved buildSelectedCard into a shared function to be called from both methods the only difference is that for regular building the spheres are validated and spent.  Nothing too crazy.  Updated the validation client-side to ensure only level 1s from the row or from filed can be selected and subsequently built (server-side validation for anti-cheat)

I also finally made the switch from a research dialog to a new row which pops up at the top of the page.  Again taking a leaf from Race for the Galaxy with their Explore action.  The controls for showing/hiding the research dialog was clunky and silly.  Really no reason to use it.  Only catch was that I then also need a different top offset for the ring on smaller screens, but this was a simple addition.

Next lets confirm the Research trigger is working and then move to the Discounts.  Yep it’s working.

Also quickly I was reading some of the docs today and I may have to do a lot of refactoring around my strings to ensure they fit nicely into the BGA translation framework. Will see. That might wait until after I submit for alpha.

Discounts were a bit tough but they seem to be fully working now.  Another case of basically needing to implement them twice: once on the client-side so it’s obvious why a gizmo can be made cheaper and again server-side for anti-cheat.  Crucially it is necessary to handle the edge case where someone actually does not want to use the discount (e.g. if they’re freeing up space for energy of a more desirable color).  Handling this by making the discount toggleable like a converter.  I considered passing the toggle status as part of the service call, but realistically the server doesn’t care if they toggled it.  Any cost in the *range* of discount\_cost \<= paid \<= cost is acceptable.  Much easier that way too.

There were some oddities around handling the ability to pay 0 energy (level 1 from file/research or level 2 with 2 discounts).  The spheres are passed as a comma separated string so PHP has to explode them to an array.  Apparently exploding an empty string yields an array with one element (empty string).  The converter validation considered this as a red energy because empty string is treated as 0 for array indexing.  The result is I kept getting a confusing error about trying to spend a red energy when building a gizmo for free.  Took a little while to understand what was going on, but an easy fix once I did.

So that should be every converter effect.  What else is there?

1. Stats \- this one is probably required pre-alpha  
2. Better translations \- probably wait  
3. Better animations \- probably wait  
4. Better responsiveness \- specifically on very wide screens.  Potentially run all player panels on the left and board on the right?  
   1. I at least centered the game  
5. ~~Better tooltips \- need to rerun the materials generation script with the improved gizmo descriptions.  Perhaps implement the various icons and their meanings~~  
6. ~~Player card information \- number of built gizmos? Number of level 3 gizmos?~~  
7. ~~Ensure that used converters show up accordingly in the UI~~  
8. ~~Change out the image for the dispenser \- overhaul display for energy row in general~~  
   1. Just removed it.  I don’t think there needs to be any icon, the row should be self-explanatory enough

Difficult to decide how “perfect” (from my perspective) I want to get it before submitting for alpha.  I know there will 100% be loads of things I don’t catch and I’m eager to get other perspectives.

## **24 Sept 2022**

Finally added stats.  Started with a bunch of basic ones.  Implementing was pretty simple.

I ran the “test” utility for the first time and it identified a good deal of things I knew about but others I didn’t.  Needed to clean up translations formatting \- remove it from unexpected errors, remove the backtick string usage since this means something else, use sprintf in php and dojo.string.substitute in JS.  I think these are looking pretty good, but I’m still not sure about the materials tooltips.  Will translators need to translate “When you pick a “ 100 times?  Or will it be smarter?  If it’s an issue, I could refactor the tooltips to be a concatenation of fewer translatable strings.  But that is definitely not a prealpha task.

Animations are still very annoying.  Picking energy jumps it to the bottom of the screen and then slides it up to the ring.  I’m not sure if there’s something different I should be doing or it’s just zones not playing nicely.

I ran through an entire test game and did see two more state-based bugs.  One around trying to buy a card and it saying I didn’t pick enough energy (this could have been timing related as well).  The second was around trying to click a converter.  I believe there was an energy in my ring that the game effectively did not know about.  My thought to ensure this doesn’t happen is to “reload” the spend\_sphere data at the start of every turn via some state args.  Should be a relatively negligible time spend to ensure accuracy.

Also adding data to the player card on number of cards built by each player along with level 3 cards built by each player since these are relevant for game end.  Regenerated the materials file and all the tooltips now look good to me now, but still have concerns around translations.

Need to add tooltips on *all* items.  I.e. the energy in the row should indicate they can be picked.  Each icon in the player cards should say what it is.  Could add state-based tooltip to the action bar telling users they can click on converters.  Color / outline buttons differently based on their behavior.  Rerun the test utility and see what else comes up.

Hit the bug with ghost energy again so it definitely needs to be fixed.  But time for bed.

## **25 Sept 2022**

Added some energy refreshing logic at the start of each turn.  However I still hit a bug where I could not select an energy when building a gizmo.  The onEnergySelect event did not fire.  So there still must be some issue, but I don’t want to spend a ton of time on it yet.

Added the rest of the tooltips \- look good to me.  I learned from the dev community on Discord that animations only work when the element being slid is position:absolute.  This immediately fixed the issue around sliding a picked energy.  I think I will refactor the energy row to be a zone.  This should make the animations work even better and show a cool sliding effect when picking just like the real life row.

Will need to spend some more time on the rest of the animations too.  Other big change is clean-up around the translations.  This article was immensely helpful: [Translations \- a recap | BGA Developers (bga-devs.github.io)](https://bga-devs.github.io/blog/posts/translations-summary/)

I have officially submitted for Alpha, but there is definitely work to be done.

## **28 Sept 2022**

I revamped the energy row and I’m much happier with it, but I don’t think it’s final.  I also refactored it to a zone.  Using weight it now auto slides energies to the right as a new energy is pushed into the row which looks very nice.  However, sliding energy from row to ring does *not* look nice.  I understand why, but not how to fix it.

1. Energy is in the energy\_row div positioned absolutely via inline style applied by the zone framework  
2. dojo.slideToObject alters the position of the energy causing it to slide from the row to the ring *but* the actual div is still a child of the energy\_row the whole time  
3. On completing the slide, the energy div is placed within the ring div, but it jumps to another side of the screen because its position is now in relation to a different parent div  
4. placeInZone then slides it to it’s appropriate position within the energy ring via zone framework

Placing within the ring prior to the slide causes the same issue.  So I need to do something smarter.

1. At step (3) what if it could be placed into the ring while simultaneously removing the inline style for absolute positioning.  Thus in theory it should not jump at all \- not sure if it’s possible to do both of those things at the same time?  
2. OR I could do some convoluted calculations to first update the positioning to appear to be in the exact same spot, but actually a child of the ring, then slide to it while already in it?  (1) sounds better.  
   

I’ve asked this on the dev Discord because I feel like it has to be easier than this

Bed.

## **2 Oct 2022**

Finally got this working with the help of a dev who pointed out a function called “attachToNewParent” which changes the parent div of an element while crucially leaving it in the same position on the screen by calculating appropriate absolute positioning.  This is what I was considering writing by hand so it was very helpful in making the whole animation look smooth.

There are several other places across the UI that I need to apply this logic.  I started with buying a card and it works nicely for buying from the row, but not from Research because the research area is hidden before the animation finishes.  Currently the research area is hidden onLeavingResaerch.  But maybe this logic can be delegated elsewhere:

1. Hide research on finishing the animation for build/file from research  
   1. Set a flag in this notification  
2. On Leaving Research, check the new flag before hiding

This works well.  Just make sure to remember to do all of the class updates *after* changing the parent so that the style changes don’t carry over.

Next I’m taking a look at the animations around converting energy.  This was a huge pain prior but I think I’m equipped with the right knowledge to get it working now.  In revisiting the code, that definitely needs some modularizing too \- specifically around moving energies to converters.

Well it’s mostly working but there’s one issue: for some reason when the energy returns to the ring and is placed in its zone, it does not get positioned correctly relative to other energy in the ring.

Aha, as usual it was a stupid minute mistake.  When I removed the energy from the ring zone prior to sliding I was passing in its integer energy ID instead of the string element ID (sphere\_X).  That was all I needed to fix to get the zone to position things correctly.  
Next: I removed the \!important styling from converter energy and need to fix bugs round it:  
![][image4]  
Also, use [BGA Studio Cookbook \- Board Game Arena](https://en.doc.boardgamearena.com/BGA_Studio_Cookbook#Attach_to_new_parent_without_destroying_the_object) instead

## **4 Oct 2022**

Saw a screenshot on dev discord of numbers nicely positioned on top of tokens/icons in player card.  Do this \- looks way better.  Make text white with black shadow.

## **6 Oct 2022**

Agenda:

1. Converted energy positioning \- looks great  
2. Player card energy layout.  Apply some greyscale or transparency if 0?  
   1. Went to Wingspan and borrowed a lot of CSS because the player cards look fantastic:  
      1. ![][image5]  
   2. I removed the font and inverted the colors (white on black) and centered on the token.  Heavily improved:  
      1. ![][image6]  
   3. I do need to do some testing around double digit numbers, but I love the opacity for 0\.  
   4. Yeah it looks bad for 2-digits, but the fix was fairly simple.  Instead of absolute positioning, put the number in a div with text-align center 100% width  
      1.   
3. More animations \- in testing I may have actually hit it all.  
   1. Need to do more with chains of converters.  
   2. There were some fixes around this, but I’m pretty happy with where it’s at.  
   3. Potential polish around:  
      1. Sliding cards to a specific position in their column based on number of existing cards  
      2. Sliding second convert energies to their exact position so they don’t jump over  
4. Translations  
5. Somehow I missed a trigger action: file.  Well let’s add it.  Another state, some more state transitions, and updates to ensure it can call the existing file ajax.

Ok lets talk translations.  Currently translators will have to translate every one of my 108 tooltips separately.  This is silly since realistically they should be able to translate \~10 tooltips, the basic actions (build, pick file, research) and the 4 colors which together should form every tooltip.

I refactored all the notifyAllPlayers methods in game php to use the clienttranslate pattern shown in the translation article.  Then I defined the strings that I think I will need in my materials for all the tooltips, but there’s a lot to think about still.  At this point I have 3 base tooltips for trigger, converter, and upgrade.  These each have their own set of args to pass in.  Do I add the args as columns to the google sheet?  Or infer these at runtime in the JS?  Sheet seems like the right approach but this is a lot of work.

## **7 Oct 2022**

Taking the middleground on translations.  I.e. I’m leaving the Google Sheet as-is and using the csv\_to\_php script to insert the appropriate arg columns that are passed to JS for tooltip building.

For the multiple color gizmos, need to do some nested bullshit.

I am either too tired or have had a bit to drink and am really struggling to wrap my head around this.  Going to write it out again then go to bed:

1. Translatable tooltip strings to in materials file  
2. I am updating the csv\_to\_php script I wrote to build out the associative arrays which are passed into the recursive translate JS function  
3. These arrays consist of:  
   1. A base string with ${x} tags  
   2. A definition for X which can be a translatable string OR an additional associative array  
      1. If the latter, then X must contain an i18n array of arg names to translate  
      2. And this can go any number of levels downward to build out lists of args (e.g. colors for Gizmos which could have 1 or 2\)  
      3. I should not need more than 2 levels deep

I think I get it, but the PHP syntax is also throwing me off and it still feels like my grasp is a bit tenuous.  Eh just keep working on it and can test.  No time constraints…

## **9 Oct 2022**

First I added energy icons into the logs because it looks nice and I’m procrastinating on the translations.

Okay so I almost have the CSV to PHP script working.  Just need a method that properly converts the associative tooltip array to the correct format to copy into another PHP file (materials).

## **10 Oct 2022**

Found that function, var\_export, and it was then a largely painless process to get format\_string\_recursive working.  The only thing I even asked about on the Discord was an initial error I got for passing a single param, an object with log and args, rather than two params, one for log and another for args.  I’m still occasionally missing the exact syntax, but it will stick over time.

Removed the rest of my backtick template strings throughout JS replacing with dojo.string.substitute or simple string concatenations.

Other things to do:

1. Ability to reorder researched cards for replacement on bottom of the deck  
   1. Need to consider how this looks UI-wise  
   2. And is research private knowledge?  
2. Slide cards to the bottom of their column to prevent the thing that happens when placing in zone after the initial slide  
3. Player preferences around confirm dialogs  
4. Better wide-screen support \- show all player cards on left and board on right

## **11 Oct 2022**

Approved for Alpha\!  Already lots of ongoing games (40 as of writing this).  I immediately opened the alpha group so anyone can join it and created a forum post.  First game I watched hit a simple bug around an unexpected trigger because I was going back and forth between underscores (trigger\_buildlevel2 vs trigger\_build\_level\_2).  Simple fix.

Some other good suggestions coming in and a bug report around missing tooltips for built gizmos.  Very exciting.  Lots of positive feedback.

Suggestion for replacing the “colored circle” energy tokens with actual marble sphere looking tokens.  Am working on this but asset building is a bit of a pain.  I’m still using MS Paint to get pixel alignments right \- should learn another tool more geared for the job.  

Yeah I went through the arduous “edit by paint” process that I’m used to making careful box selections and then dragging until I have a niceish square image.  It actually looks very good:  
![][image7]  
Big improvement over the previous tokens.  The major thing I learned is that for circular tokens it doesn’t matter if the circle is edged on any sides because CSS is going to round out the edges via border-radius 100%.  This makes the process a bit easier.

Also added info in the player card for archived gizmos / limit, total energy / limit, and research quantity.  Custom assets for all 3 built using a mix of paint and gimp.  Starting to feel a bit more comfortable in gimp which is good.  Fixed a bug around maintaining tooltips for built and filed gizmos (assignToNewParent / animations remove them).

Most concerning ask at this point is improving the energy dispenser design.  I want to leverage the publisher assets for this somehow, but it’s not at all obvious how given all I have are the foldable cardboard cutouts for a 3D asset.

Ok in thinking about it more I should be able to crop the F\_front image and use it as the background for the row which should be a huge improvement.  May be able to crop the “wings” as well.

## **13 Oct 2022**

This diary may lapse a little bit as I spend more time responding to bugs.  But yeah I should keep track of the things I’m fixing for posterity:

1. Yesterday there was a bug because my end game condition checked for \== 100% game progression instead of \>= and it’s possible to build 2 Gizmos in one turn using specific triggers.  Kind of a funny issue  
2. There has been a bit of confusion around how to use converters which was more or less to be expected.  But I can still guide players into how to use the system I’ve created.  The main pain points:  
   1. When you select a Gizmo, the energy in your ring is automatically highlighted for purchasing it.  If you then use a converter, you are overpaying for the Gizmo and must explicitly deselect an energy from your ring.  That’s silly so I wrote some code that automatically deselects excess energy in your ring  
   2. When you click on a converter outside of the card selected state, a confusing error was thrown.  I made this error more friendly “Must select a Gizmo before converting”  
3. I swapped the logic for discounting research and filed cards so players were unable to use their discounts (easy fix)  
4. I removed what I thought was some extraneous code that sets the build button disabled state at the bottom of toggleConverter when in fact it ensures that starting a conversion launching the color selection does not turn the button active  
5. And now I’m procrastinating because I need to split my stylesheet into two for older mobile phone compatibility.  Actually I came here to get a link to my spritesheet generator.  Let’s do it.  
   1. First (kind of) arbitrary decision is how do I divide up the Gizmos?  They need to be relatively similar sizes for efficiency but splitting down the middle of a level feels wrong.  Fortunately after adding in the “mixed” card that’s used in the player card, I have exactly 113 \= 64 (8x8) \+ 49 (7x7) card images.  This felt too serendipitous not to do.  And I did end up splitting Level II somewhere after 228\. This will complicate the updates to my CSS generation a little but who cares  
   2. Not too complicated.  Hardest part is I decided to add automatically creating the deck / mixed card / start card CSS which are all one-offs as opposed to the rest of the decks which are looped  
   3. Had a simple off by one error when switching sheets but otherwise went smoothly.  
   4. Compressed the spritesheets using a docs recommended tool ([https://squoosh.app/](https://squoosh.app/)) and it worked way better (higher compression rate) than what I did previously with gimp.  And everything now seems to be working with the new 2-sheet approach  
6. Played a test game and deployed

## **17 Oct 2022**

I did a few more deploys this weekend with some bug fixes and enhancements: tooltips for each segment of the player track (for readable text), styling of converters column to indicate usage, improved player card styling, handling of researching when deck is low, deck size number shown on each deck.

Today some more bugs to fix.  Could not loadBug on this [bug link](https://boardgamearena.com/bug?id=73960). Reproduce manually.  Card IDs: 328, 305, 308 filed.

Couldn’t reproduce.  Asked user for clarification.

Fixed a simple bug around the any to any converter where it was deselecting itself when used for a one-cost Gizmo due to some bad code.

## **18 Oct 2022**

Spent the rest of last night working on maintaining the order of researched cards when returned to the bottom of the deck.  Up until now I had spent about 0 time thinking about the order of cards in each deck.  The important part was just that after researching, the cards go back to the bottom which seemed to be working.  On closer inspection, it seems that every time the research goes back to the deck they are all assigned position 0\.  I *think* this may mean that the order retrieved is more or less random after several researches have occurred on a deck which is definitely a problem.  A refactor is already needed since a player should be able to choose the order of returning their researched cards to the bottom of the deck.  Design overview:

1. Add left/right arrows on researched cards which update their order of appearance  
   1. I did refactor the research layout and definitely need to update a few places to refer to research\_outer instead of the cards div itself  
2. On any state change/ajax from a research state, capture this order and pass it as a param to the server  
3. Update the location\_args on the researched cards:  
   1. Retrieve the location\_arg for bottom card of the deck (helper function extreme position in Card framework)  
   2. Iterate over the research card ids list, decrement arg, update  
      1. Ensure that the order indicated to users matches the actual order that code is doing this.  It’s not necessarily intuitive whether the first card is on top or bottom of the stack returned to the bottom of the deck  
4. When research is complete, the correct positions are already assigned to the cards.  Ensure that the card method that maintains position is used when returning to deck (not currently the case)

I got (1) and most of (2) working, but the server-side code needs updating.  Very happy with the styling around the left/right buttons \- struggled with this for a bit.

Fixed the issues around showing/hiding research and implemented the rest of the reordering.  Difficulties first around trying to do a bulk update on all the research gizmo location args.  Never got the query format quite right so just scrapped it and did a separate update per card.  Efficiency gains would probably be negligible for the 3-10 cards users are researching.

Second issue was around getting the cards to sort correctly in the UI such that reloading/changing states maintains the order passed into the DB.  Key was to pass the location\_arg, put the gizmo\_ids in an array, and then sort by location\_arg.

## **20 Oct 2022**

Took a break yesterday.  No major bug reports.  Barring something emerging, my next steps are:

1. I noticed the research sort arrows do not appear correctly on my phone \- need to fix  
2. Player preferences  
   1. Most important one is a confirm dialog for energy selection.  I like the idea of making it default to confirm for touch screens and the Discord community happened to be discussing it today:  
      1. window.matchMedia(‘(hover:none)’).matches;  
   2. Need to read through the docs on user preferences \- seems pretty straightforward  
   3. Also add confirm for draw triggers?  Sure, think if anything else.

For now I have only added it for Picking Energy.  Works great.  Also fixed a bug around researching an empty deck because the extremePosition returned null.  Restyled buttons to stand out better.

## **25 Oct 2022**

Just bug fixes today.  Edge case improvements:

1. A player believed that the count of built Gizmos was not updating in their player card.  In actuality it was only the final build that did not update.  I noticed it was the Build Level I for free trigger and reproduced the issue.  The notification that updates the player card was only triggering when spending energy.  Easy fix once found  
2. Someone’s very complex chain of conversions was failing with an error about one of the colors they were converting not existing.  Took a lot of logging, reading, and thinking to catch it, but effectively the double any converts mistakenly inherited the same color for both converts when in reality it could convert to 2 different colors for doubling.  Again easy fix once I found it  
3. Next the behavior around rapidly clicking converters while building can sometimes error out forcing a refresh before being allowed to perform any action.  Ideally when someone selects a converter, I want the UI to “lock” such that any other clicks do nothing until that conversion / animation are complete.  So that’s what I did.  Added a lock flag around the onclicks and animations.  At the start of any onclick, if either lock is true, return.  Disable the locks once animation and onclick are complete  
4. Another game completely stopped working after they selected a card from their archive.  Trying to Cancel yielded a repeated error about triggerSelect not being an allowed action.  On diving into their DB, I noticed that triggering\_multiple\_uses was set to one.  This is used to handle the number of activations for draw\_3 and pick\_2 triggers.  It *should* always be reset between states but somehow it wasn’t.  No clue how, but I added an additional check in the Cancel function to ensure that there is also a triggering gizmo for the multiple uses state to apply effectively preventing this in the future.  I made sure to do extra testing around those triggers to ensure they work as expected

Deployed all of those fixes.  I came to the decision today that I will implement a commonly asked for suggestion: showing the color of the next marble.  Although not explicitly written into the rules, it can be seen on the real life track very easily such that in my friendly games we made this an explicit house rule instead of “trying not to look at it”.  That is likely going to be my next improvement.

Other possibilities:

1. Animations for refilling the row \- perhaps try to use Thoun’s new component  
2. Wide screen support to put player cards next to rows DONE  
3. More precise slideToObjectPos usage based on already present elements; Nah like this as-is  
4. ?

## **5 Feb 2023**

Gizmos has been in production for over a month.  There are still bug reports trickling in, but by and large it works well and is getting a lot of players.  Biggest hurdle earlier in Jan was I made a good deal of mistakes with my translatable strings. Specifically some of them were being translated server-side so players saw strings in the turn-players language rather than their own.  I also made some silly mistakes with i18n declarations in materials.  I think they are all working now.

Other improvements:

1. Adding a player preference to auto-pass if all remaining triggers are not usable (full energy ring, full archive, cannot file/research upgrades)  
   1. This logic is fairly straightforward but raises another issue: triggered but not usable gizmos should not have a green outline.  Maybe a different color?  Yep did this
